---
title: "Assembly layers structuring biodiversity"
author: "Gabriel Munoz-Acevedo"
format: revealjs
editor: visual
---

## Understanding Earth's living systems

-   To protect nature

-   To protect human well being

-   To respond to environmental challenges

-   To understand how life works

climate → traits → interactions → networks → functioning → people.

## How are Earth’s living systems organized?

-   The ecological assembly framework

-   What we have learned from the ecological assembly framework

-   Common assumptions

    -   equilibrium / stationarity

    -   trait–interaction mapping is stable

    -   interactions are pairwise & context-independent

    -   contemporary gradients approximate selective environments

    -   space substitutes for time

    -   sampling/observation processes don’t bias inference

-   Challenges moving forward

## How climate change reshapes biodiversity?

-   Earth climate dynamics as driver of ecological assembly process

    -   Climate as an ecological filter

    -   Climate modifies species interactions

-   Uneven responses across regions & taxa

-   Historical assembly processes

> Biodiversity reorganization under climate change is predictable when we model ecological assembly as a trait-mediated, interaction-structured process operating across trophic levels and constrained by historical climate dynamics

## Thesis chapters

-   Chapter overview

## Chapter 1: Trait based inference of ecological network assembly

-   Ecological networks are shaped by how species interact and connect

-   Network assembly processes within and between trophic levels

-   Species traits are central to network assembly processes

-   Simulating network assembly

-   Inferring network assembly with null models

## Chapter 2: Distinct functional responses of consumers and their producers to climate drive mutualistic network assembly

-   Environmental filtering shapes species trait diversity unvenly across trophic levels generating FTA

-   Questions:

    -   How does functional asymmetry vary across large-scale environmental gradients?

    -   Does it have an influence in network structure?

-   Network assembly workflow

    -   Data sources

    -   Inferring network assembly

        -   Evaluating models of network assembly

        -   Defining interaction guilds

        -   Predicting interaction guilds

        -   Defining co-concurrence probabilities

    -   Assembling networks

    -   Climatic data

-   Results

    -   How climate change influence FTA

    -   How FTA relates to network structure

-   Interpretation

## Chapter 3: Climate change drove the deep time assembly of biodiversity

-   Why deep time?

    -   The problem with inferring ecological assembly processes with contemporary climatic gradients

-   How does historical climate change dynamics influence the genesis of species pools

-   Climate change across the Neogene

    -   Mean atmospheric temperature

-   Fossil datasets

-   Defining dissimilarity of fossil paleoassemblages

-   Delineating fossil paleobiogeographic regions

-   Linking climate change to taxonomic replacement

## Implication towards predicting the future of biodiversity

## Implication towards biodiversity conservation

## Implication towards climate change adaptation

## Implication towards understanding transformation of ecosystems
